# Secure NestJS Drizzle Template
> Repository: https://github.com/RamosJSouza/secure-nestjs-drizzle-template
> Website & Portfolio: https://ramosdainformatica.com.br/
> LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ramos-souza/
> GitHub: https://github.com/RamosJSouza
> Email: ramosinfo@gmail.com

## Description
Production-grade, security-first NestJS 11 boilerplate for regulated industries — Fintech, Healthtech, and Enterprise SaaS. Passes security reviews, penetration tests, and compliance audits (SOC 2, GDPR, LGPD, PCI-DSS Req 8) out-of-the-box. Requires Node.js >= 24.0.0.

Maintained by **Ramos de Souza Janones** — Senior Full Stack Engineer specializing in secure, high-availability backend architectures (NestJS, Drizzle, PostgreSQL, Redis) for multi-tenant SaaS, fintech, and healthtech.

---

### Professional Highlights
- **Fintech / PIX (Bradesco via Wipro):** APIs and mobile integrations for Brazil's instant payment system — 1M+ daily transactions, 99.9% uptime, Bacen regulatory compliance.
- **Healthtech (Digitalcare2u):** End-to-end health platform — async Kafka architecture, PostgreSQL/MongoDB, production observability, technical leadership and mentoring.
- **SaaS B2B Multi-tenant (Limify / ReDraw):** Production SaaS for architecture studios — NestJS 11, Drizzle ORM, RBAC 100% DB-driven, JWT RS256, Argon2id, PostgreSQL RLS, Stripe subscriptions, BullMQ, 85%+ test coverage, Trivy container scans.
- **Enterprise Frontend (Darede):** React 17→18 migration at scale — build time −28.9%, hot reload −60%, bundle −9.5%, zero production regressions.
- **AI & LLM Engineering:** Audits and refactors LLM-generated codebases; integrates OpenAI/Groq/HuggingFace; applies Cursor, MCPs, and agent skills with production-grade engineering standards.
- **Cloud & DevOps:** AWS, GCP, Azure, Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Sentry, Datadog.

### Newsletter (LinkedIn)

---

## Tech Stack
- Framework: NestJS 11 (Node.js >= 24.0.0 — LTS Krypton)
- Database: PostgreSQL + Drizzle ORM (type-safe, migration-driven — no schema sync in production)
- Email: Nodemailer (Ethereal auto-account in dev; SMTP in production) via Ports & Adapters (`IEmailProvider` / `MailFacade`)
- Caching & Queues: Redis — JTI blocklist, RBAC permission cache, opaque recovery tokens, credential stuffing counters, BullMQ webhook delivery
- Auth: JWT RS256 asymmetric keys + Argon2id password hashing (transparent bcrypt migration)
- Infrastructure: Docker multi-stage builds, non-root user `nestjs`, Healthcheck via wget
- CI/CD: GitHub Actions (lint → type-check → coverage ≥ 85% → npm audit → Docker + Trivy scan)
- Weekly: CodeQL, Snyk, Trivy FS scan (SARIF → GitHub Security tab); Dependabot for npm/Actions/Docker
- Husky pre-commit: npm audit --audit-level=moderate blocks commit on vulnerabilities

---

## Core Security Features

### Authentication & Session Management
- JWT RS256: asymmetric signing; private key never shared
- Access token payload: `{sub, jti, typ}` only — no email, roleId, or PII
- Refresh token payload: `{sub, jti, typ}` with rotation + reuse detection
- JTI revocation: O(1) Redis GET per request via `TokenRevocationService`
- Session limits: max 10 concurrent; oldest evicted with JTI revocation
- Device fingerprinting: SHA-256(User-Agent + IP) per session
- Explicit logout + currentPassword on change-password

### Account Recovery & Email Verification (NEW)
- **Opaque tokens only — never JWT** in email links
- `POST /auth/forgot-password`: always HTTP 202 (anti-enumeration); timing-normalized response; dummy Argon2 on miss path
- Token: `randomBytes(32)` hex → SHA-256 hash stored in Redis (15 min TTL); raw token only in email
- `POST /auth/reset-password`: atomic burn-after-read (Lua GET+DEL); Argon2id; revokes all sessions + JTIs via `revokeAllActiveUserSessions()`
- `POST /auth/send-verification` + `POST /auth/verify-email`: double opt-in; sets `users.emailVerifiedAt`
- `@RequireEmailVerification()` + `EmailVerificationGuard`: blocks unverified users on sensitive routes
- `GracePeriodGuard`: blocks sensitive actions for 24h after `passwordChangedAt` (configurable)
- Demo route: `POST /users/sensitive-action` (JWT + verified email + grace period clear)
- Email: `MailModule` → `NodemailerAdapter` (Ethereal preview URL logged in dev)

### Password Security
- Argon2id (64 MiB / 3t / 4p); transparent bcrypt migration
- Password policy: min 8, max 72, uppercase + lowercase + digit

### Risk Engine
- 5 parallel signals; critical (≥80) blocks login + revokes all sessions

### RBAC (Database-driven)
- Feature → Permission → RolePermission → Role → User
- `RbacModule` @Global; permission cache with `RBAC_CACHE_TTL`
- `PermissionGuard` + `@RequirePermissions('feature:action')`

### Multi-Tenancy
- `TenantDatabaseService.withTenant()` + optional PostgreSQL RLS
- Example: `docs/examples/rbac-multi-tenant.md`

### Credential Stuffing, HTTP Hardening, Rate Limiting, Observability, Webhooks
(See docs/en/security.md for full detail)

---

## Key Architecture Decisions
- `DatabaseModule`, `SecurityModule`, `TenantModule`, `RbacModule` are `@Global()`
- `AppCacheModule` via `SecurityModule` — Redis or in-memory (`DISABLE_REDIS=true`)
- `AuthModule` imports `MailModule`, `AuthGuardsModule`; opaque token store via `OpaqueTokenStorePort` (Redis or in-memory)
- `OrganizationsModule` is placeholder (schema only)
- Users schema: `emailVerifiedAt`, `passwordChangedAt` (migration `0004`)
- Shared helpers: `hashOpaqueToken()`, `revokeAllActiveUserSessions()`, `opaqueTokenKey()`

---

## Test Coverage
- **97 unit tests** across **21 suites** (`src/**/*.spec.ts`)
- E2E: `test/app.e2e-spec.ts`, `test/feature.e2e-spec.ts`, `test/tenant-isolation.e2e-spec.ts`
- CI minimum coverage: **85%**

---

## Docs Structure
- `docs/en/` — English (architecture, authentication, rbac, security, observability, configuration, compliance, deployment)
- `docs/pt-br/` — Portuguese translations
- `docs/examples/rbac-multi-tenant.md` — Full RBAC + RLS example
- `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-11-account-recovery-email-verification.md` — Implementation plan

---

## Environment (account recovery)
```
APP_URL=http://localhost:3000
SMTP_HOST= SMTP_PORT=587 SMTP_USER= SMTP_PASS=
SMTP_FROM_EMAIL= SMTP_FROM_NAME=
PASSWORD_RESET_TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS=900
EMAIL_VERIFICATION_TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS=86400
PASSWORD_CHANGE_GRACE_PERIOD_HOURS=24
FORGOT_PASSWORD_MIN_RESPONSE_MS=250
```

---

## Quick Start
```bash
cp .env.example .env
npm install --legacy-peer-deps
openssl genrsa -out private.pem 2048 && openssl rsa -in private.pem -pubout -out public.pem
npm run db:migrate && npm run seed:rbac
npm run dev
# Swagger: http://localhost:3000/api/docs (dev only)
# Ethereal email preview URL logged to console on forgot-password / send-verification
```
